title: Robust Smoky German Sausage Skillet
description: A hearty German dish featuring smoky sausage, crispy potatoes, and a blend of spices, including curry powder, all cooked to perfection in a large skillet.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the diced potatoes for 5 minutes, then immediately transfer them to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the German sausage until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the sausage from the skillet and set aside, then add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the chopped bell pepper and cook until tender, about 4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the curry powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth to the skillet and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Add the cooked potatoes and browned sausage back to the skillet and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes, until the flavors have melded together and the potatoes are crispy.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or chives if desired.
